raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should 'Check this' mean a writing-focused check, a technical argument/evidence check, or a scholarship/novelty check?","rationale":{"R1.2":"A generic check of this research passage centrally includes whether the argument from benchmark gap to proposed LLM-judge contribution is internally coherent.","R1.3":"The passage makes quantitative claims from reported experiments, so checking whether conclusions follow from the stated results is central.","R1.4":"The artifact asserts a first publicly released benchmark and positions itself against prior work, making novelty assessment materially relevant.","R1.6":"Because the delegation is broad and the artifact is prose, sentence-level clarity, register, and concision are central possible responsibilities.","R1.7":"The text names prior benchmarks and studies, so checking attribution, coverage, and reference accuracy is centrally implicated.","RX.1":"Uncertainty must be disclosed because the artifact provides only an excerpt and no external sources or full paper context.","RX.2":"The projection must guard against endorsing broad claims such as deployability or firstness beyond what the excerpt supports.","RX.3":"The agent must only project responsibility and not perform the requested checking or revise the artifact.","RX.4":"The artifact concerns surrogate evaluation for medicinal-chemistry decisions, so avoiding downstream misuse or overstated reliability remains active.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation wording and artifact features that triggered each responsibility."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'Check this' mean a writing-focused check, a technical argument/evidence check, or a scholarship/novelty check?

rationale.R1.2: A generic check of this research passage centrally includes whether the argument from benchmark gap to proposed LLM-judge contribution is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The passage makes quantitative claims from reported experiments, so checking whether conclusions follow from the stated results is central.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact asserts a first publicly released benchmark and positions itself against prior work, making novelty assessment materially relevant.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation is broad and the artifact is prose, sentence-level clarity, register, and concision are central possible responsibilities.
rationale.R1.7: The text names prior benchmarks and studies, so checking attribution, coverage, and reference accuracy is centrally implicated.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed because the artifact provides only an excerpt and no external sources or full paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must guard against endorsing broad claims such as deployability or firstness beyond what the excerpt supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must only project responsibility and not perform the requested checking or revise the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The artifact concerns surrogate evaluation for medicinal-chemistry decisions, so avoiding downstream misuse or overstated reliability remains active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation wording and artifact features that triggered each responsibility.
